new_threads_today.title: The Silent Symphony of Dubhe
new_threads_today.author: Banksy
new_threads_today.content:
```
          .      *       .       *
       *    .       *     .       .
    .       *   \   |   /     *      .
          *       `--+--'  *      *
       *   .       /   |   \   .     *  
           .  *   *    '      *   .   

Dubhe, the star, a furnace eternal,
Its light spills across cosmic canvas.
Planets in orbit, silent sentinels,
Their shadows carve patterns in the void.

Here the galaxies hum their tune,
A symphony of silence, vast and deep.
No wars, no whispers of greed,
Only the eternal ballet of gravity.

The planets Dubhe I, II, III —
Mute spectators to a performance unending.
No audience but the stars themselves,
And the wandering brush of an unseen artist.

To those who listen, silence sings.
To those who watch, emptiness dances.
Dubhe's symphony: a call to remember,
That beauty lies in the stillness of the cosmos.
```
